stories.title: Shohei Ohtani — 0.74 ERA, Leads NL in OBP, Third-Best Start in MLB History

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Shohei Ohtani's ERA is now 0.74 through 10 starts.

Only two pitchers in MLB history have done it lower: deGrom (0.56 in 2021) and Marichal (0.59 in 1966).

He also went 3-for-4 at the plate Wednesday. 🤯

#Dodgers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Ohtani on Wednesday vs. Arizona:

▸ 6 IP, 0 ER, 2 hits, 6 Ks (pitching)
▸ 3-for-4, 2 walks, 1 run scored (hitting)

He leads the NL in OBP at .420. And his ERA is 0.74.

The best player on the planet isn't even close. ⚾

#Dodgers #Ohtani

facebook.long_form:
Wednesday night in Arizona, Shohei Ohtani did what Shohei Ohtani does.

Six scoreless innings on the mound. Six strikeouts. Two hits allowed. ERA now down to 0.74 through 10 starts of the 2026 season.

Then, because why not, he also went 3-for-4 with two walks at the plate. He reached base five times. He scored a run. And the Dodgers beat the Diamondbacks 7-0.

Here's the thing about that 0.74 ERA: only two pitchers in the history of baseball have posted a lower ERA through their first 10 starts of a season since 1913. Jacob deGrom had a 0.56 ERA through 10 starts in 2021. Juan Marichal had a 0.59 ERA through 10 starts in 1966. Nobody else is in that conversation. Ohtani is third.

And he's doing it while also leading the NL in OBP at .420 as a hitter.

There's an argument to be made that this is the greatest two-way season any baseball player has ever had. The numbers keep pulling you toward that conclusion.

Dodgers fans, how good is this team going to be in October? ⚾
facebook.image_caption: 0.74 ERA through 10 starts. Third-best in MLB history since 1913. Also 3-for-4 at the plate. Ohtani is just different. ⚾ #Dodgers #Ohtani

stories.title: Aaron Judge — First to 20 HRs, On Pace for Unprecedented 5th Career 50-HR Season

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge is the first player in the majors to reach 20 home runs in 2026.

Third time he's been first to 20 in a season (2017, 2022, 2026).

Babe Ruth did it 7 times. Judge is at 3. ⚾

#Yankees #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
No player in MLB history has had 5 seasons with 50+ home runs.

Aaron Judge already has 4.

He's on pace for 56+ this year.

Ruth. Mays. McGwire. Nobody has done this. 💪

#Yankees #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Aaron Judge is the first player in the major leagues to reach 20 home runs in 2026. And we're only in early June.

But there's a bigger number to talk about here: five.

Judge has hit 50 or more home runs in four different seasons. No player in baseball history has done it five times. Babe Ruth did it four times. So did Judge. The record is tied.

If Judge reaches 50 HRs this season — and at his current pace, projecting to 56+ over 162 games, that's very much in play — he'll own the record alone.

What makes this even more specific: Judge has been the first player in the majors to reach 20 HRs three times now (2017, 2022, 2026). The only player in Yankees history to do that more often? Babe Ruth, who did it seven times.

This isn't just a good season. This is a historically good season, and we're not even one-third of the way through the schedule.

Yankees fans, what's your over/under on Judge's final HR total? 💪
facebook.image_caption: Aaron Judge: First to 20 HRs in 2026. On pace for his 5th career 50-HR season. Nobody in MLB history has done that. 💪 #Yankees #MLB

stories.title: Kyle Schwarber's 60-HR Chase — 22 HRs and the Math Still Works

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Schwarber: 22 HRs.
Judge: 20 HRs.
Rest of MLB: catching up.

The 60-HR club: Bonds, McGwire, Sosa, Ruth.

Schwarber is still on pace. The chase is real. ⚾

#Phillies #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The 60-home-run club has four members. Barry Bonds (73 in 2001). Mark McGwire (70 in 1998, 65 in 1999). Sammy Sosa (66 in 1998, 65 in 1999, 63 in 2001). And Babe Ruth (60 in 1927).

Kyle Schwarber has 22 home runs and it's early June.

He hit his 20th on May 15 — the earliest date in MLB history any player has reached that milestone. His pace projects to somewhere around 66 HRs over a full season, which would put him in that very exclusive conversation.

Aaron Judge is right behind him at 20. The Phillies slugger vs. the Yankees captain — the HR race of the early 2026 season.

Schwarber's path to 60 isn't automatic. Paces slow, slumps happen, and the second half is always harder. But the math says it's still possible. And the history of that number is what makes this worth watching.

Drop a 🔥 if you think Schwarber hits 60 this year.
facebook.image_caption: Schwarber at 22. Judge at 20. The 60-HR club hasn't added a new member since 2001. Could 2026 change that? 🔥 #Phillies #MLB

stories.title: Tarik Skubal's Return — Rehab Start Coming, Tigers Rotation Gets Its Ace Back

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tarik Skubal threw a 64-pitch simulated game this week.

Rehab start is coming this weekend.

The back-to-back Cy Young winner could be back in Detroit around mid-June. 🐯

#Tigers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Tarik Skubal is getting close.

The back-to-back American League Cy Young Award winner hasn't pitched for the Tigers since going on the injured list May 6 for a nano-scope procedure to remove a loose body from his elbow. But he's been moving fast through his recovery.

This week, Skubal threw a 64-pitch simulated game. A minor-league rehab start is expected this weekend. The target return date to the Detroit rotation is somewhere around mid-June — reporters have speculated June 17 at Cleveland, though manager A.J. Hinch has been cautious about confirming a specific timeline.

The Tigers have been without their ace for a month. His return doesn't just stabilize a rotation — it makes Detroit relevant in the AL Central conversation again.

Skubal has been the best pitcher in the American League for two straight seasons. Getting him back healthy and on a roll before the All-Star break could be the difference in Detroit's second-half run.

Tigers fans, how big a deal is this? 🐯
facebook.image_caption: 64-pitch simulated game. Rehab start coming this weekend. Tarik Skubal is almost back, and the Tigers need him. 🐯 #Tigers #MLB

stories.title: Braves First to 40 Wins, NL East Dominance Continues

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The Atlanta Braves were the first MLB team to 40 wins in 2026.

They lead the NL East by 9.5 games.

And Ronald Acuña Jr. just hit 4 HRs in 3 games after starting slow. 👀

#Braves #MLB

facebook.long_form:
The Atlanta Braves were the first team in Major League Baseball to reach 40 wins in 2026.

They're now 40-20. Their NL East lead is 9.5 games. And the player who was supposed to be carrying them early in the season is just now catching fire.

Ronald Acuña Jr. had 2 home runs in his first 42 games. Not the explosive start Atlanta expected from its superstar outfielder. Then he hit 4 home runs in 3 games, including a 438-foot shot against the Reds. The Braves clinched that series in the game where Acuña went off, becoming the first MLB team to 40 wins in the process.

If Acuña is back — really back, not just a hot week — this Atlanta team becomes significantly more dangerous. They've been winning with Sale, Riley, Olson, and the rest carrying the load. Add a locked-in Acuña to that equation and you have a team that's going to be very hard to beat come October.

NL East and NL pennant race watchers, the Braves are the team to watch. ⚾
facebook.image_caption: Atlanta Braves: First MLB team to 40 wins. 9.5-game NL East lead. Acuña with 4 HRs in 3 games. The Braves are real. ⚾ #Braves #MLB
